differential_diagnoses: Mycotic aortic aneurysm with impending rupture
Infective endocarditis with aortic root abscess
Septic embolization (e.g., to coronary arteries or distal vasculature)
Paraspinal or vertebral osteomyelitis with epidural abscess
Staphylococcus aureus bacteremia with metastatic foci
Acute coronary syndrome (secondary to sepsis or embolic phenomenon)
next_steps: Perform urgent Transesophageal Echocardiography (TEE) to evaluate for vegetations and valvular function
Obtain emergent CT angiography of the chest and abdomen to reassess the aorta for mycotic aneurysm progression or dissection
Consult cardiothoracic surgery for evaluation of potential aortic involvement
Consult infectious disease for optimization of antistaphylococcal therapy and duration
Obtain repeat blood cultures to confirm clearance of MSSA bacteremia
Perform neurological assessment and possibly MRI of the thoracic spine to rule out epidural abscess in the setting of persistent bacteremia and focal pain
